name: Wheat Rust
symptoms: Orange-yellow powdery pustules on leaves
Leaves turn yellow and wither
Stems weaken and lodging occurs
symptoms_hi: पत्तियों पर नारंगी-पीले रंग के पाउडर जैसे धब्बे
पत्तियां पीली पड़कर सूख जाती हैं
तने कमजोर हो जाते हैं और फसल गिर जाती है
symptoms_guj: પાંદડા પર નારંગી-પીળા રંગના પાવડર જેવા ટપકા
પાંદડા પીળા પડી જાય છે અને સુકાઈ જાય છે
થડ નબળા પડી જાય છે અને પાક ઢળી પડે છે
causes_hi: हवा द्वारा फैलने वाले कवक बीजाणु

treatments.detail: Remove and burn all infected plant material immediately
Spray at 2g per liter of water covering all leaf surfaces
Mix 5ml neem oil per liter water, spray every 7 days

treatments_hi.detail: सभी संक्रमित पौधों को तुरंत हटा दें और जला दें
2 ग्राम प्रति लीटर पानी में मिलाकर पत्तियों पर स्प्रे करें
5 मिली नीम का तेल प्रति लीटर पानी में मिलाकर हर 7 दिन में स्प्रे करें

treatments_guj.detail: બધા ચેપગ્રસ્ત છોડને તરત જ દૂર કરો અને બાળી નાખો
2 ગ્રામ પ્રતિ લિટર પાણીમાં ભેળવીને પાંદડા પર છંટકાવ કરો
5 મિલી લીમડાનું તેલ પ્રતિ લિટર પાણીમાં ભેળવીને દર 7 દિવસે છંટકાવ કરો

preventionTips_hi: रतुआ-प्रतिरोधी गेहूं की किस्मों का उपयोग करें
पौधों के बीच उचित दूरी बनाए रखें
हर मौसम में फसल चक्र अपनाएं
organicAlternative: Neem oil spray 5ml/L every 7 days acts as natural fungicide
organicAlternative_hi: नीम के तेल का स्प्रे (5 मिली/लीटर) हर 7 दिन में प्राकृतिक कवकनाशी के रूप में कार्य करता है
organicAlternative_guj: લીમડાના તેલનો છંટકાવ (5 મિલી/લિટર) દર 7 દિવસે કુદરતી ફૂગનાશક તરીકે કામ કરે છે
weatherConditions: Cool and humid weather 15-22°C with morning dew
weather_hi: सुबह की ओस के साथ 15-22°C का ठंडा और आर्द्र मौसम
weather_guj: સવારના ઝાકળ સાથે 15-22°C ઠંડુ અને ભેજવાળું વાતાવરણ
firstAidTip: Remove infected leaves immediately to slow spread
firstAid_hi: प्रसार को धीमा करने के लिए संक्रमित पत्तियों को तुरंत हटा दें
firstAid_guj: ફેલાવો ધીમો કરવા માટે ચેપગ્રસ્ત પાંદડા તરત જ દૂર કરો

name: Wheat Smut (Karnal Bunt)
symptoms: Partially bunted grains with fishy smell
Blackish-brown spore masses in grain
Reduced grain weight and quality
symptoms_hi: मछली जैसी गंध वाले आंशिक रूप से सड़े हुए दाने
अनाज में काले-भूरे रंग के बीजाणु समूह
अनाज के वजन और गुणवत्ता में कमी
symptoms_guj: માછલી જેવી ગંધવાળા આંશિક રીતે સડેલા દાણા
causes_hi: फूल आने के समय ठंडी और नम स्थिति
causes_guj: ફૂલ આવતી વખતે ઠંડુ અને ભેજવાળું વાતાવરણ

treatments.detail: Treat seeds with Carboxin 37.5% + Thiram 37.5% at 3g/kg
Plow infected fields deep to bury infected debris
Apply Trichoderma viride at 4g/kg seed as bio-control

treatments_hi.detail: बीजों को कार्बोक्सिन 37.5% + थिरम 37.5% (3 ग्राम/किग्रा) से उपचारित करें
संक्रमित कचरे को दबाने के लिए खेतों की गहरी जुताई करें
जैव-नियंत्रण के रूप में ट्राइकोडर्मा विरिडी (4 ग्राम/किग्रा बीज) लगाएं

treatments_guj.detail: બીજને કાર્બોક્સિન 37.5% + થીરમ 37.5% (3 ગ્રામ/કિલો) થી માવજત આપો
ચેપગ્રસ્ત કચરાને દાટી દેવા માટે ખેતરોમાં ઊંડી ખેડ કરો
જૈવિક-નિયંત્રણ તરીકે ટ્રાઈકોડર્મા વિરીડી (4 ગ્રામ/કિલો બીજ) નો ઉપયોગ કરો

preventionTips_hi: प्रमाणित रोगमुक्त बीजों का उपयोग करें
प्रत्येक बुवाई से पहले बीजों का उपचार करें
संक्रमित पौधों को हटा दें और जला दें
preventionTips_guj: દરેક વાવણી પહેલા બીજની માવજત કરો
ચેપગ્રસ્ત છોડને દૂર કરી બાળી નાખો
organicAlternative: Trichoderma viride seed treatment (4g/kg) prevents infection
organicAlternative_hi: ट्राइकोडर्मा विरिडी बीज उपचार (4 ग्राम/किग्रा) संक्रमण को रोकता है
organicAlternative_guj: ટ્રાઈકોડર્મા વિરીડી બીજ માવજત (4 ગ્રામ/કિલો) ચેપ અટકાવે છે
weatherConditions: Cool moist weather during flowering stage
weather_hi: फूल आने के दौरान ठंडा और नम मौसम
weather_guj: ફૂલ આવવા સમયે ઠંડુ અને ભેજવાળું વાતાવરણ
firstAidTip: Do not use infected grain as seed next season
firstAid_hi: अगले सीजन में संक्रमित अनाज को बीज के रूप में उपयोग न करें
firstAid_guj: પછીની સિઝનમાં ચેપગ્રસ્ત દાણાનો બીજ તરીકે ઉપયોગ ન કરો

name: Wheat Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery coating on leaf surface
Leaves turn yellow underneath coating
Reduced photosynthesis and growth

treatments.detail: Dust wettable sulphur 25kg/ha on infected plants
Spray Propiconazole 25 EC at 1ml/L water
Spray 1 tsp baking soda + 1 drop soap per liter water

organicAlternative: Dilute baking soda spray weekly prevents powdery coating
weatherConditions: High humidity 70-80% with 15-20°C temperature
firstAidTip: Remove heavily infected leaves and improve air circulation

name: Wheat Leaf Blight
symptoms: Brown oval spots with yellow margins on leaves
Spots enlarge and merge causing leaf death
Premature leaf drop reducing yield

treatments.detail: Collect and burn all infected plant debris after harvest
Apply Mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/L water every 10 days
Apply neem cake 250kg/ha to suppress soil-borne inoculum

organicAlternative: Neem cake soil treatment reduces fungal spores in soil
weatherConditions: Warm moist conditions after rain, 25-30°C
firstAidTip: Remove infected leaves and avoid wetting foliage

name: Cotton Boll Rot
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on bolls turning brown
Bolls fail to open, remain hard and rotted
White or pink fungal growth on boll surface
causes: High humidity and rainfall during boll formation

treatments.detail: Hand pick all infected bolls and destroy them away from field
Apply Copper oxychloride 50% WP at 3g/L water
Open drainage channels to reduce standing water and humidity

preventionTips: Ensure proper plant spacing for air circulation
Avoid excessive irrigation during boll stage
organicAlternative: Copper-based Bordeaux mixture (1%) spray as organic fungicide
weatherConditions: High humidity above 80% with frequent rain
firstAidTip: Remove infected bolls before fungus spreads to healthy ones

name: Cotton Leaf Curl Virus
symptoms: Leaves curl upward and thicken
Veins thicken and darken becoming prominent
Plant stunted with severe yield loss
causes: Virus spreads from infected surrounding crops
No direct soil or seed transmission

treatments.detail: Spray Imidacloprid 17.8 SL at 0.5ml/L water to kill vector
Uproot and burn plants with more than 50% infection
Place 10-15 yellow sticky traps per acre to monitor and trap whiteflies

organicAlternative: Neem seed kernel extract 5% spray repels whitefly vectors
weatherConditions: Hot dry conditions favor whitefly population explosion
firstAidTip: Spray insecticide to kill whiteflies and stop virus spread

name: Cotton Fusarium Wilt
symptoms: Leaves turn yellow and wilt suddenly
Brown discoloration inside stem when cut
Plant collapses and dies within days

treatments.detail: Apply Carbendazim 50% WP at 2g/L water around plant base
Make raised beds and open drainage channels
Apply Trichoderma harzianum at 2.5kg/ha mixed with FYM

organicAlternative: Trichoderma harzianum bio-agent suppresses Fusarium in soil
weatherConditions: Warm temperatures 28-32°C with excess soil moisture
firstAidTip: Remove wilted plants with roots and avoid spreading soil

name: Cotton Alternaria Blight
symptoms: Circular to irregular dark brown spots on leaves
Spots have concentric rings like a target
Premature defoliation in severe cases

treatments.detail: Spray Mancozeb 75% WP at 2g/L water every 10 days
Thin out plants to improve air circulation and reduce humidity
Spray 5% garlic extract solution as organic fungicide

organicAlternative: 5% garlic extract spray acts as natural fungicide weekly
weatherConditions: High relative humidity above 75% with warm temperatures
firstAidTip: Remove and destroy spotted leaves before spores spread

name: Cotton Pink Bollworm
symptoms: Flowers fail to open (rosette formation)
Entry holes in bolls with pink caterpillars inside
Damaged seeds and lint quality reduction

treatments.detail: Apply Spinosad 45 SC at 0.3ml/L water targeting bolls
Set 5 pheromone traps per acre to trap male moths
Spray neem oil 3ml/L water as deterrent on cotton bolls

organicAlternative: Neem oil 3ml/L spray plus pheromone traps reduces infestation
weatherConditions: Warm dry weather favors moth flight and egg laying
firstAidTip: Install pheromone traps immediately to monitor moth population

name: Groundnut Tikka Disease
symptoms: Circular brown spots with yellow halos on leaves
Both early (yellow halo) and late blight spots
Premature defoliation reduces pod fill
causes: Cercospora arachidicola and C. personatum fungi

treatments.detail: Apply Chlorothalonil 75% WP at 2g/L water
Remove infected leaves and plant debris from field
Apply fermented sour whey diluted 1:5 as organic treatment

preventionTips: Apply fungicide preventively in humid seasons
organicAlternative: Sour whey spray (1:5 dilution) weekly inhibits fungal growth
weatherConditions: Humid conditions above 80% RH with temperatures 25-30°C
firstAidTip: Remove heavily spotted leaves at first sign to slow spread

name: Groundnut Rust
symptoms: Small orange-brown pustules on lower leaf surface
Yellowing of leaves around pustules
Premature leaf drop weakening plant

treatments.detail: Apply Propiconazole 25 EC at 1ml/L water fortnightly
Remove alternate rows to improve air circulation
Mix 5ml neem oil + 1tsp turmeric powder per liter water

preventionTips: Apply preventive fungicide before peak humidity
organicAlternative: Neem oil 5ml + turmeric 2g per liter water weekly spray
weatherConditions: Humid above 80% RH with 25-30°C after rain
firstAidTip: Remove and burn infected leaves at first orange spot

name: Groundnut Stem Rot
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions at stem base near soil
White mycelium with mustard-seed sclerotia
Entire plant collapses and dies
symptoms_hi: मिट्टी के पास तने के निचले हिस्से पर पानी से भीगे हुए धब्बे
सफेद कवक (माइसेलियम) और सरसों के दाने जैसे दाने (स्क्लेरोटिया)
पूरा पौधा मुरझाकर गिर जाता है और मर जाता है
symptoms_guj: જમીનની નજીક થડના તળિયે પાણી જેવા ડાઘા
સફેદ ફૂગ (માયસેલિયમ) અને રાઈના દાણા જેવા સફેદ ટપકા (સ્ક્લેરોશિયા)
આખો છોડ કરમાઈને ઢળી પડે છે અને સુકાઈ જાય છે
causes_hi: स्क्लेरोटियम रोल्फसी नामक मिट्टी का कवक
जलभराव और मिट्टी का उच्च तापमान
causes_guj: પાણી ભરાઈ રહેવું અને જમીનનું ઊંચું તાપમાન

treatments.detail: Drench soil with Carbendazim 50% WP 2g/L around plant base
Uproot all collapsed plants with surrounding soil and destroy
Incorporate mustard cake 500kg/ha into soil before planting

treatments_hi.detail: पौधे के आधार के पास मिट्टी में 2 ग्राम/लीटर कार्बेंडाजिम 50% WP डालें
सभी मुरझाए हुए पौधों को आसपास की मिट्टी के साथ उखाड़कर नष्ट कर दें
बुवाई से पहले 500 किग्रा/हेक्टेयर सरसों की खली मिट्टी में मिलाएं

treatments_guj.detail: છોડના થડ પાસે જમીનમાં 2 ગ્રામ/લિટર કાર્બેન્ડાઝીમ 50% WP નાખો
ઢળી પડેલા બધા છોડને આસપાસની માટી સાથે ઉખાડી નાશ કરો
વાવણી પહેલા 500 કિગ્રા/હેક્ટર રાઈનો ખોળ જમીનમાં ભેળવો

preventionTips: Deep plow to expose and destroy sclerotia
preventionTips_hi: बीजाणुओं को नष्ट करने के लिए गहरी जुताई करें
अनाज के साथ 2 साल का फसल चक्र अपनाएं
preventionTips_guj: ફૂગના દાણા (સ્ક્લેરોશિયા) નાશ કરવા ઊંડી ખેડ કરો
ધાન્ય પાકો સાથે 2 વર્ષનું પાક ચક્ર અપનાવો
organicAlternative: Trichoderma viride 2.5kg/ha in FYM suppresses Sclerotium in soil
organicAlternative_hi: गोबर की खाद में 2.5 किग्रा/हेक्टेयर ट्राइकोडर्मा विरिडी मिट्टी में संक्रमण रोकती है
organicAlternative_guj: છાણિયા ખાતર સાથે 2.5 કિગ્રા/હેક્ટર ટ્રાઈકોડર્મા વિરીડી જમીનમાં ચેપ અટકાવે છે
weatherConditions: Hot humid soil above 30°C with water-logging
firstAidTip: Remove collapsed plants with all soil and avoid spreading

name: Groundnut Bud Necrosis Virus
symptoms: Necrotic (dead) buds and growing tips
Ring spots and line patterns on leaves
Severe stunting and no pod formation
causes: Thrips transmit Tomato Spotted Wilt Virus
Peak infestation during dry hot periods

treatments.detail: Spray Imidacloprid 17.8 SL at 0.5ml/L or Spinosad at 0.3ml/L
Rogue out all symptomatic plants from field
Use silver reflective plastic mulch to repel thrips

organicAlternative: Kaolin clay spray 5% repels thrips without chemicals
weatherConditions: Hot dry conditions favor thrips population buildup
firstAidTip: Immediately spray insecticide to stop thrips and roguing infected plants

name: Rice Blast
symptoms: Diamond-shaped gray lesions with dark borders on leaves
Neck rot causing panicles to snap
Empty or partially filled grains

treatments.detail: Apply Tricyclazole 75% WP at 0.6g/L water on leaves and panicles
Stop nitrogen fertilizer and do not apply during humid periods
Apply Pseudomonas fluorescens 10g/L water as bio-control agent

organicAlternative: Pseudomonas fluorescens foliar spray induces plant resistance
weatherConditions: High humidity with cool nights and warm days 24-28°C
firstAidTip: Stop nitrogen fertilizer and spray fungicide at neck emergence

name: Rice Bacterial Leaf Blight
symptoms: Water-soaked yellow stripes along leaf margins
Leaves dry from tip turning straw-colored
Bacterial ooze visible on cut stem in water
causes: Flooding and strong winds spread bacteria

treatments.detail: Drain field completely and avoid re-flooding for 7 days
Apply Copper hydroxide 77% WP at 2g/L water
Mix Streptomycin sulfate 0.5g + Copper oxychloride 2g per liter

organicAlternative: Copper sulfate 0.2% spray acts as natural bactericide
weatherConditions: Flooding and strong winds during warm humid weather
firstAidTip: Drain the field immediately and stop nitrogen fertilizer

name: Rice Sheath Blight
symptoms: Oval greenish-gray lesions on leaf sheath near water
Lesions enlarge with brown borders
Entire sheath and leaves die in severe cases
causes: High nitrogen and warm stagnant water

treatments.detail: Apply Hexaconazole 5% EC at 2ml/L water on lower stem and water surface
Thin overcrowded areas to allow air movement
Mix Trichoderma harzianum 250g/ha into irrigation water

organicAlternative: Trichoderma harzianum in irrigation water reduces soil inoculum
weatherConditions: Warm temperatures 28-34°C with high relative humidity
firstAidTip: Drain field to reduce water surface contact with stem

name: Rice Brown Spot
symptoms: Circular to oval brown spots on leaves
Spots have yellow halos and gray centers
Infected grains show dark brown discoloration

treatments.detail: Apply MOP (Muriate of Potash) at 40kg/ha to correct deficiency
Apply Mancozeb 75% WP at 2g/L water on diseased plants
Treat seeds with hot water 53°C for 10 minutes then dry before planting

organicAlternative: Wood ash soil application improves potassium and reduces disease
weatherConditions: Hot humid conditions with nutrient-poor or water-stressed soil
firstAidTip: Apply potassium fertilizer immediately as most cases are deficiency-related

name: Tomato Early Blight
symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings on lower leaves
Yellow area around spots causing leaf drop
Collar rot at soil level on stem

treatments.detail: Prune all infected lower leaves and dispose away from field
Apply Chlorothalonil 75% WP at 2g/L water every 7-10 days
Apply 1 tbsp baking soda + 1 tsp dish soap per gallon water weekly

preventionTips: Stake plants to improve air circulation
Mulch around base to prevent soil splash
organicAlternative: Copper-based Bordeaux mixture (1%) spray every 10 days
weatherConditions: Warm humid weather 24-30°C with alternating wet and dry periods
firstAidTip: Remove infected lower leaves immediately and mulch the base

name: Tomato Late Blight
symptoms: Water-soaked gray-green lesions on leaves
White mold on leaf underside in humid conditions
Greasy brown rot on fruits spreading rapidly
causes: Spreads extremely fast in favorable conditions

treatments.detail: Apply Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP at 2.5g/L immediately
Pull up all heavily infected plants and bury or burn
Apply Copper hydroxide 77% WP at 2g/L as protective fungicide

preventionTips: Apply fungicide preventively before cool humid weather
organicAlternative: Copper hydroxide spray every 7 days provides good protection
weatherConditions: Cool humid weather 10-20°C with prolonged leaf wetness
firstAidTip: Spray systemic fungicide IMMEDIATELY — can destroy entire crop in 3 days

name: Tomato Leaf Curl Virus
symptoms: Leaves curl upward and become thick leathery
Yellowing of leaf margins between veins
Severe stunting with no or poor fruit set
causes: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus via whitefly
Rapid spread in hot dry conditions

treatments.detail: Spray Imidacloprid 17.8 SL at 0.5ml/L to control whitefly vector
Rogue out all severely infected plants from field
Use silver polyethylene mulch to repel whiteflies

preventionTips: Apply neem oil to repel whitefly
organicAlternative: Neem oil 5ml/L spray every 7 days repels whitefly vectors
weatherConditions: Hot dry conditions 35-40°C favor whitefly outbreak
firstAidTip: Control whitefly immediately, no cure exists for virus once infected

name: Tomato Fusarium Wilt
symptoms: One side or all leaves wilt during the day and may recover at night
Brown discoloration of vascular tissue when stem is cut
Plant progressively yellows and dies
causes: Fusarium oxysporum f.sp. lycopersici in soil

treatments.detail: Drench soil with Carbendazim 50% WP 2g/L around plant base
Uproot entire plant with roots and soil, do not compost
Cover moist soil with clear plastic for 6 weeks in summer to kill fungus

organicAlternative: Trichoderma viride soil application 2.5kg/ha in compost before planting
weatherConditions: Warm soil 25-30°C with poor drainage and low soil pH
firstAidTip: Remove plant immediately with roots — no cure, prevent spread

name: Tomato Mosaic Virus
symptoms: Mosaic light and dark green patches on leaves
Leaves become crinkled and distorted
Fruits show internal browning and poor color
causes: ToMV and TMV viruses spread by contact and tools
Workers touching infected then healthy plants

treatments.detail: Uproot all symptomatic plants immediately and dispose
Wash tools and hands with 10% bleach or soap between plants
Silver mulch reduces aphid vectors that spread some strains

organicAlternative: Skimmed milk spray (1:10) on tools and hands inactivates TMV virus
weatherConditions: Any season — spread by mechanical contact not weather-dependent
firstAidTip: Wash hands with soap immediately after touching infected plants

name: Onion Purple Blotch
symptoms: Water-soaked white lesions that turn purple-brown
Lesions have yellow borders and cause leaf tip die-back
Severe infection collapses leaves and reduces bulb size
causes: Alternaria porri fungus in humid conditions
Prolonged leaf wetness after rain or dew
Dense planting and poor air circulation

treatments.detail: Apply Iprodione 50% WP at 2g/L water every 7-10 days
Ensure raised beds and good drainage to reduce leaf wetness
Mix garlic extract 3% + neem oil 5ml/L as organic fungicide

organicAlternative: Garlic extract 3% + neem oil 5ml per liter water weekly spray
weatherConditions: Humid conditions above 70% with morning dew on leaves
firstAidTip: Remove infected leaf tips and avoid wetting leaves when irrigating

name: Onion Stemphylium Blight
symptoms: Small yellow to tan spots that turn brown
Spots have water-soaked to dry texture
Tip blighting and eventual leaf death

treatments.detail: Spray Spinosad 0.3ml/L to reduce thrips which create infection entry points
Apply Mancozeb 1.5g + Carbendazim 0.5g per liter water
Spray neem oil 5ml/L to repel thrips and reduce fungal spreading

organicAlternative: Neem oil 5ml/L spray controls thrips and reduces Stemphylium risk
weatherConditions: Humid weather with frequent rain and thrips activity
firstAidTip: Control thrips immediately as they create entry wounds for the fungus

name: Onion Downy Mildew
symptoms: Pale green to yellow areas on leaves
Violet-gray fuzzy growth under humid conditions
Leaves collapse and dry out in warm weather
causes: Cool moist nights 10-15°C with warm days

treatments.detail: Apply Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP at 2.5g/L water
Improve drainage and air circulation in field
Irrigate only during morning so leaves dry before night

preventionTips: Apply preventive fungicide before cool humid weather
organicAlternative: Copper sulfate 0.3% spray as protective treatment in cool weather
weatherConditions: Cool nights below 15°C with high humidity and morning dew
firstAidTip: Irrigate in morning only and ensure good air flow through canopy

name: Potato Late Blight
symptoms: Dark water-soaked lesions on leaves turning brown
White fuzzy mold on undersides of leaves
Tubers show brown rotted patches internally
causes: Infected seed tubers and crop debris

treatments.detail: Apply Ridomil Gold 68 WG at 2.5g/L water immediately
Remove all infected haulms and dispose off-field
Apply Copper oxychloride 50% WP at 3g/L as protectant

preventionTips: Apply fungicide before cool humid weather arrives
organicAlternative: Copper oxychloride 50% WP spray every 7 days provides good organic protection
weatherConditions: Cool humid weather 10-20°C with >90% humidity and rain
firstAidTip: Spray systemic fungicide IMMEDIATELY — can wipe out crop in one week

name: Potato Early Blight
symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings on lower leaves
Yellowing around spots causing leaf drop
Reduces leaf area and tuber fill

treatments.detail: Remove and destroy all spotted lower leaves
Apply Chlorothalonil 75% WP at 2g/L every 10 days
Apply compost or farmyard manure to strengthen plant immunity

organicAlternative: Strong compost application improves plant health and resistance
weatherConditions: Warm humid weather 25-30°C with alternating wet and dry spells
firstAidTip: Remove all spotted lower leaves to significantly slow spread

name: Potato Black Scurf
symptoms: Black irregular sclerotia stuck on tuber skin
Stem canker causing stem base browning
Reduced emergence of sprouts
causes: High soil moisture and cool temperatures

treatments.detail: Soak seed tubers in Carbendazim 0.1% solution for 30 minutes before planting
Allow soil to warm above 10°C before planting to reduce disease
Apply Trichoderma viride as seed piece dust before planting

organicAlternative: Trichoderma viride seed piece treatment suppresses Rhizoctonia naturally
weatherConditions: Cool moist soil below 15°C with high moisture
firstAidTip: Treat seed tubers with fungicide before planting — best prevention

name: Corn Downy Mildew
symptoms: Pale green to yellow stripes on young leaves
White downy growth on both leaf surfaces
Plants stunted with poor ear development
causes: Wet weather during early plant growth
Oospores in infected soil and seed

treatments.detail: Treat seeds with Metalaxyl 35 WS at 6g/kg seed before sowing
Uproot all infected young plants to stop spread
Apply Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP at 2.5g/L on remaining plants

organicAlternative: Trichoderma harzianum seed + soil treatment reduces Peronosclerospora
weatherConditions: Wet rainy weather during seedling and early vegetative stage
firstAidTip: Remove all infected seedlings immediately before spores spread

name: Corn Northern Leaf Blight
symptoms: Long tan to gray elliptical lesions 2-15cm on leaves
Lesions have wavy margins feeling soft and papery
Starts on lower leaves moving upward

treatments.detail: Apply Propiconazole 25 EC at 1ml/L water at tassel emergence
Plow deep after harvest to bury infected cobs and stalks
Apply neem oil 5ml/L water as preventive foliar spray

preventionTips: Apply fungicide at tassel stage in humid years
organicAlternative: Neem oil 5ml/L spray as preventive foliar treatment
weatherConditions: Moderate humid weather 18-27°C with heavy dew
firstAidTip: Apply fungicide at tassel stage to protect ear for maximum yield protection

name: Corn Smut
symptoms: Large grayish-white galls on ears, tassels, and leaves
Galls rupture releasing black powdery spores
Infected ears fail to develop properly
causes: Injuries to plant from hail, insects, or cultivation
Warm dry weather with intermittent rain

treatments.detail: Cut off all galls before they turn black and dispose in plastic bags
Treat seeds with Carboxin + Thiram 3g/kg to reduce soil-borne inoculum
Avoid cultivation injuries near plants especially at tasseling

organicAlternative: Remove galls promptly — most effective non-chemical management
weatherConditions: Warm dry conditions with occasional rain causing plant stress
firstAidTip: Remove galls in plastic bags before they turn black and release spores

name: Mustard White Rust
symptoms: White blister-like pustules on lower leaf surface
Distorted flowers and siliqua malformation
Leaves yellow and eventually drop

treatments.detail: Apply Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP at 2g/L water
Remove and destroy all infected flower stalks showing white pustules
Apply Copper oxychloride 50% WP 3g/L as protective spray

preventionTips: Monitor fields in cool humid weather
organicAlternative: Copper oxychloride 3g/L spray every 10 days during vulnerable stage
weatherConditions: Cool foggy mornings 10-18°C with high humidity
firstAidTip: Spray fungicide at bud formation stage before white pustules appear

name: Mustard Alternaria Blight
symptoms: Dark brown circular spots with concentric rings on leaves
Infected siliqua (pods) turn brown and shriveled
Premature shattering of infected siliqua
causes: Alternaria brassicae and A. brassicicola spores
Warm humid conditions after pod formation

treatments.detail: Treat seeds with Thiram 75% WS at 3g/kg seed
Apply Iprodione 50% WP at 2g/L water at flowering and pod stage
Apply diluted butter milk 1:5 with water as organic preventive

preventionTips: Apply two sprays at flowering and pod stage
Rotate with non-brassica crops for 2 years
organicAlternative: Butter milk 1:5 dilution spray weekly reduces Alternaria infection
weatherConditions: Warm humid conditions during flowering and pod filling stage
firstAidTip: Apply fungicide at flowering to protect pods at most critical stage

name: Nitrogen Deficiency
symptoms: Uniform yellowing of older lower leaves first
Yellowing moves upward as deficiency worsens
Stunted growth with pale green overall color
causes: Sandy soils with low organic matter

treatments.detail: Top-dress with Urea at 50-65 kg/ha in evening or on cloudy days
Incorporate well-decomposed farmyard manure 10t/ha before sowing
Spray 2% urea solution on leaves for quick uptake

preventionTips: Conduct soil testing before each season
Add compost to improve soil organic matter
organicAlternative: Green manure crops or compost application maintains long-term nitrogen
weatherConditions: Sandy soils during heavy rain season with leaching
firstAidTip: Apply 2% urea foliar spray for immediate uptake response in 3 days

name: Iron / Zinc Deficiency
symptoms: Yellowing between veins on young upper leaves (interveinal chlorosis)
Young leaves turn pale yellow-white while veins stay green
Stunted growth and small leaves
causes: Calcareous soils in Gujarat and Rajasthan

treatments.detail: Drench soil with Ferrous sulfate 0.5% solution around plants (for Fe)
Apply Zinc sulfate 25kg/ha to soil or spray 0.5% foliar (for Zn)
Apply sulfur 200kg/ha to lower pH in alkaline soils

preventionTips: Apply zinc sulfate regularly in alkaline soils
organicAlternative: Compost with neem leaves improves micronutrient availability in soil
weatherConditions: High pH alkaline soils common in Gujarat arid zones
firstAidTip: Spray 0.5% Zinc sulfate or Ferrous sulfate foliar for quick green-up

name: Potassium Deficiency
symptoms: Scorching or burning of leaf tips and margins turning brown
Older leaves affected first, brown edge with green center
Weak stems, poor fruit quality and small size
causes: Sandy light soils with low potassium

treatments.detail: Apply Muriate of Potash (KCl) 40-60 kg K2O/ha to soil
Spray 1% KNO3 or K2SO4 solution on leaves for fast response
Incorporate wood ash 500kg/ha which is rich in potassium

preventionTips: Include potassium in base fertilizer every season
Add organic matter to improve potassium retention
organicAlternative: Wood ash 500kg/ha supplies significant potassium naturally
weatherConditions: Sandy soils in heavy rainfall areas prone to potassium leaching
firstAidTip: Apply foliar 1% KNO3 spray for immediate response within a week

name: Magnesium Deficiency
symptoms: Interveinal chlorosis on older middle-age leaves
Green veins surrounded by yellow tissue in a fish-bone pattern
Leaves may turn purple or red in some crops
causes: Sandy acid soils low in magnesium
High potassium application blocking magnesium uptake

treatments.detail: Apply Epsom salt (MgSO4) 10-15kg/ha to soil or 2% foliar spray
Apply dolomitic limestone which contains calcium and magnesium
Avoid excess potassium fertilizer which locks out magnesium

preventionTips: Use dolomite lime instead of regular lime
Apply Mg sulfate as annual top-dressing
organicAlternative: Dolomitic lime application provides sustained magnesium release
weatherConditions: Sandy acid soils with high rainfall promoting leaching
firstAidTip: Spray 2% Epsom salt (MgSO4) on leaves for visible response in 5-7 days

name: Aphid Infestation
symptoms: Clusters of tiny green, black or yellow insects on new growth
Leaves curl and distort
Sticky honeydew secretion causing sooty mold growth
causes: Warm dry weather with soft new growth
Natural enemies absent due to pesticide use

treatments.detail: Dislodge aphids from plants using strong jet of water
Apply Imidacloprid 17.8 SL at 0.5ml/L water covering undersides of leaves
Spray neem oil 5ml/L + few drops dish soap per liter water

preventionTips: Use reflective mulch to disorient aphids
organicAlternative: Neem oil 5ml/L + soap spray every 5 days effectively controls aphids
weatherConditions: Warm dry weather 22-30°C with high plant nitrogen content
firstAidTip: Blast aphids off with strong water jet — simple but very effective

name: Whitefly Infestation
symptoms: Clouds of tiny white flies disturb when plants shaken
Yellowing and upward curling of leaves
Honeydew secretion leading to black sooty mold
causes: Bemisia tabaci population explosion in hot dry weather

treatments.detail: Install 10-15 yellow sticky traps per acre immediately for monitoring and trapping
Apply Diafenthiuron 50% WP at 1g/L water on leaf undersides
Spray neem oil 5ml/L water targeting leaf undersides every 7 days

preventionTips: Remove alternate host weeds from field borders
organicAlternative: Neem oil 5ml/L spray every 7 days significantly reduces whitefly populations
weatherConditions: Hot dry conditions 35-45°C with low rainfall
firstAidTip: Install yellow sticky traps immediately and check every 2 days

name: Spider Mite Infestation
symptoms: Tiny yellow or white speck dots on leaf surface from feeding
Fine webbing visible on undersides of leaves
Leaves turn bronze, yellow and drop — plant dehydrates
causes: Tetranychus urticae thriving in hot dry conditions
Suppression of natural predators by pesticides

treatments.detail: Spray strong water jet on leaf undersides to dislodge and kill mites
Spray Abamectin 1.8 EC at 0.5ml/L water as specific miticide
Apply neem oil 5ml/L water targeting leaf undersides every 5 days

preventionTips: Maintain plant hydration — stressed plants attract mites
Avoid pyrethroid pesticides that kill natural predators
Apply overhead irrigation in hot dry weather
organicAlternative: Neem oil 5ml/L spray every 5 days effectively controls spider mites
weatherConditions: Very hot dry weather above 35°C with low humidity—peak conditions for mites
firstAidTip: Spray strong water jets on leaf undersides daily for 3 days to break lifecycle
